ab: a lralrak mi ziya kaazu mi i Mulivelivek, a temakesi mi lra muasalr mi mukuwa kazuwa i Tawyuan, kazu mi i tokay muvaaw.
ch: 小時候我們家住在初鹿，上學以後，就搬到了桃園，一直在都市裡生活。
en: When we were young, our family lived in Mulivelivek. After I started school, we moved to Taoyuan and have been living in the city ever since.

ab: temakesi ku lra kana daygak, mukuwa ku menadanadama za mikireeman kana kakuwayanan kana Yuancumin, malrazam ku lra kana Yuancumin i Taiwan ulra tu kakuwayanan a marekamelrimelri.
ch: 上了大學以後，我修了一堂關於原住民文化的課程，瞭解到臺灣原住民族有各式各樣豐富的文化。
en: After I went to college, I took a course on aboriginal culture and learned that the aboriginal people in Taiwan have various and rich cultures.

ab: semeneng, mukasa ku kani ivalri na ma’inayan mukuwa kiyumalra kana dawdawan i zekalr. maranger mi malrazam za naniyam kukuwayanan.
ch: 因此，今年暑假時，我和哥哥回到部落訪問老人家，想要瞭解自己族群的文化。
en: So this summer, my brother and I went back to our tribe to visit the elderly, hoping to understand the culture of our own ethnic group.

ab: walu pulu’ lra tu ’ami na dawdawan, mawmu, maresan maralralrak ziya nu mena’u ta.
ch: 老人家已經八十歲了，但是看起來還像中年人。
en: The old man is already eighty years old, but he still looks like a middle-aged man.

ab: kiyumalr ku kanintaw za sayhu u za emaeman ngai. dalus nu temarapinuyumayan i intaw. sayhu temarabunun, temarapayrang, zi temaradipung.
ch: 我問他會哪些語言，他的卑南語說得很流利，也會說布農語、閩南話和日本話。
en: I asked him which languages he knew, and he was fluent in pinuyumayan, as well as Bunun, Hokkien and Japanese.

ab: ku kiyumalray i intaw mu, malrazam ku lra za a lralrak ziya i intaw mu menanadam za ngai za Dipung. a Bunun tu ’azin, malrazam i intaw za sazu a Payrang i kiyakarunan zi sayhu merengay za kemani sazu za ngai.
ch: 我問他才知道，原來他小時候就學日本話，太太是布農族人，而他工作上認識許多閩南人，所以他會說這麼多語言。
en: I asked him to find out that he learned Japanese when he was a child, his wife is an Bunun, and he knows many Hokkien at work, so he can speak so many languages.

ab: mazayazayar mi mu, merengay na dawdawan za sazu a kinadaman za vulrtrengan za pa’alrupan.
ch: 我們訪談的時候，老人家談論了許多關於狩獵的傳統知識。
en: During our interview, the old man talked about a lot of traditional knowledge about hunting.

ab: ara ’azi patulru’ay mi malrazam naniyam kiningeran zi, ulra naniyam soci na semasing. maruwa mi ziya kingkinger.
ch: 雖然我們沒有辦法完全聽懂，但是我們有用手機錄影，可以再仔細聆聽。
en: Although we could’t fully understand it, we could record it on our cell phones and listen to it more.

ab: ’azi ku kamalrazam zanta kakuwayanan a lralrak ku ziya. maira ku saema kaninta Yuēncumin.
ch: 小時候我不瞭解自己族群的文化，對於自己是原住民有點不好意思。
en: When I was young, I didn’t understand the culture of my ethnic group, and I was a little embarrassed that I was an aborigine.

ab: nu kamalrazaman ku sazu lra mu, marakatatena’ a pinuyumayan ku, a kinipilimi’an ku, zi a trau za mamatras.
ch: 瞭解愈多以後，愈覺得自己身為卑南族人，是很獨特的，也很值得驕傲。
en: The more I know, the more I feel that being a pinuyumayan is unique and something to be proud of.
